signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short immediately. This is a fresh re-entry that is already underwater after less than a minute, has no stop, and liquidation distance is only about 0.99%, which is not enough room to defend a countertrend short inside a live short-squeeze regime. Position management comes before any new idea here.

BTC remains structurally bid despite this local pullback, but the board is still under a live geopolitical VETO tied to the Hormuz squeeze backdrop. That makes fresh action lower quality than simply managing the open SOL risk first.

ETH is still holding a strong recovery structure, but it is also directly exposed to the same event-driven squeeze regime and already well off the lows. Under position management, it is not cleaner than just flattening the bad SOL short.
LDO has rebounded, but funding is positive and the move is already extended enough that it is not a clean fresh long into the live veto regime. It stays watch-only.
XPL is still holding above its recovery base, but it is a thinner continuation name inside an event-sensitive tape. That is not a better active trade than simply removing the weak SOL short.
PNUT is already off the lows and now fading from its squeeze extension, which makes both chase-longs and fresh fades messy under the live event brake. Best left alone.
MORPHO is one of the cleaner underlying structures on the sheet, but it still does not have enough independent edge to override the live VETO backdrop. Watch it, do not force it.
EIGEN is recovering but already trades like a lower-quality bounce with positive funding and event-sensitive execution risk. There is no reason to prioritize it over flattening the live SOL risk.
TST still has squeeze mechanics underneath, but it is already extended and very unstable after the explosive move and open-interest expansion. Under a live veto, that stays firmly watch-only.
KNEIRO still lacks robust price context in this snapshot and remains too thin and underdefined to trade confidently in an event-driven tape. No action.
